Guide June 12, 2026 · 12 min read

China Trademark Monitoring and Protection Strategies

Trademark registration is only the beginning — ongoing monitoring and protection are essential to maintain your brand rights in China. This guide covers monitoring strategies, enforcement options, and how to protect your trademark from infringement and squatting.

🛡️

Key Takeaway

Trademark registration is only the beginning — ongoing monitoring and protection are essential to maintain your brand rights in China. This guide covers monitoring strategies, enforcement options, and how to protect your trademark from infringement and squatting.

⚠️ Why Monitoring is Essential

🎯

Common Threats

  • • Copycat products flooding the market
  • • Competitors using similar marks
  • • Online infringement on e-commerce
  • • Trademark squatting attempts
💰

Business Impact

  • • Lost revenue and market share
  • • Brand dilution and confusion
  • • Customer trust erosion
  • • Legal costs and distractions
💸 The Cost of Not Monitoring
10-30%
Lost Revenue
¥50K-500K
Recovery Costs
2-5 Years
Total Disruption

👁️ Trademark Monitoring Strategies

1

Official CNIPA Monitoring

Monitor trademark gazette for similar marks, track new applications in your classes, identify potential conflicts early.

ℹ️ RTMCN's Service:

Professional monitoring team, automated alerts, strategic analysis, opposition filing and management.

2

E-Commerce Platform Monitoring

Monitor Taobao, JD.com, Pinduoduo, Douyin, and international platforms for unauthorized use and counterfeit products.

3

Social Media Monitoring

Monitor WeChat, Weibo, Xiaohongshu, Douyin for brand mentions, unauthorized accounts, and misleading content.

4

Domain Name Monitoring

Monitor similar domain registrations, typosquatting attempts, and new TLD registrations.

⚖️ Enforcement Strategies

🏛️

Administrative Enforcement

File complaint with local AIC for quick action against clear infringement cases.

⚖️

Civil Litigation

File lawsuit in IP court for complex cases, significant damages, and injunction relief.

🚔

Criminal Prosecution

Report to public security bureau for large-scale counterfeiting and organized crime.

🌐

Online Enforcement

File platform complaints for e-commerce infringement, social media violations, and domain disputes.

Need Professional Monitoring?

Don't wait until infringement damages your brand. Get proactive monitoring and protection from RTMCN to safeguard your trademark in China.

Get Monitoring Assessment Schedule Consultation

This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Consult with a licensed trademark agent for guidance specific to your situation.
